description: In an apartment, Chandler and Phoebe stand behind Ross, who holds an object made of a troll doll attached to a wooden block. An uncertain speaker says, "For."
Ross continues to examine the object in his hands.
Ross says, "Okay. Good."
The setting changes to an outdoor park covered in autumn leaves. Ross walks forward carrying the object, followed by Chandler and Rachel. Monica, wearing a blue Giants sweatshirt, walks over.
Monica points at the object and says, "Okay. Where the hell did you get that? When mom and dad drove you to the hospital to get your nose fixed I swam into the lake"
Monica points to her chest and continues, "and fished it out."
Ross gestures toward his chest with his hand and says, "That cup is mine."
Monica points at Ross and says, "No, it's not. You want it, you're going to have to win it." Phoebe, standing nearby, asks, "All right. So are we not having dinner at all?"
Phoebe walks away from the group toward a blonde woman, Margha, who stands near the park fence.
Phoebe asks, "Hey, could you? Could you please step timer, Margha." Margha, holding a small object, replies, "Okay, beefy." Phoebe's smile drops slightly.
Monica claps her hands and walks toward Phoebe and Joey. Joey holds a football.
Monica motions with both hands and says, "Come on. Let's go. Come on. It's time to get serious. Huddle up."
Monica turns to face Joey.
Monica points her finger at Joey and says, "Joey, keep your head in the game."
Joey looks toward Margha.
Joey gestures toward Margha, who is sitting on a bench with a dog, and says, "It's hard, you know? His huddle's close to the Dutch girl."
Monica raises her hand.
Monica says, "All right, look. If I take Chandler out of the running, will you be able to focus?" Joey asks, "What are you going to do?" Monica gestures and says, "You just make sure he"
"catches the ball. I'll take care of the rest." Joey nods and says, "Okay." Joey claps and says, "Come on. Come on," as they move to their positions.
Chandler runs forward across the leaves.
Joey winds up to throw and says, "Go, Chandler. I got it. Come on, come on. Come on." Chandler catches the ball, runs past Margha, and says, "Hi."
Monica runs up behind Chandler and grabs him around the upper body.
As Monica tackles Chandler, an uncertain speaker says, "Look at her."
Monica and Chandler fall hard onto the leaf-covered ground. Monica gets up quickly while Chandler remains on his back.
Monica laughs, points at Chandler, and says, "Tackled by a girl."
Chandler gets up as Joey walks over saying, "Guess you don't see that every day, do you?" Ross walks toward them with his hands raised and asks, "Whoa, whoa, whoa. What's with the tackling?"
Monica shrugs, holding her hands open, and says, "What? I just touched him, and he went over."
Ross steps closer to Monica, leaning in, and says, "Okay, you want to play rough? We can play rough."
Ross says, "Let's get ready to run ball," his voice dropping in pitch. He shakes his head slightly.
An upbeat dance song begins playing. Chandler hikes the football to Joey, who receives it and steps back to throw.
Joey throws the ball. Chandler catches it and dives into the leaves. Margha claps her hands from the bench.
The song lyrics "Ready for this?" are heard as Joey pulls Chandler up from the ground.
Chandler stands over a fallen player. Joey throws a pass, and Ross jumps to intercept it but misses, allowing Monica to catch the ball behind him.
Ross grabs Monica around the waist from behind, but she breaks his grip and continues running with the ball.
Ross holds the football above his head with both hands while Phoebe grabs him around the waist from behind.
The players continue running across the field. Rachel stands away from the action near a brick wall, holding a pretzel.
Rachel stands still, chewing. She tosses a small piece of food into the air and catches it in her mouth.
Chandler prepares to hike the ball. Monica and Phoebe rush toward him, and Phoebe grabs the ball directly from his hands.
Phoebe runs across the field holding the ball, laughing while she runs.
A voice in the song says, "Go."
Phoebe throws the ball hard into the leaves, then jumps up and down with her arms raised.
Phoebe claps and says, "I love this game."
The upbeat music stops. Ross stands with his hands on his hips. Rachel walks over to the group, holding her pretzel.
Ross gestures to Rachel and asks, "Hey, where'd you get that?" Rachel takes a bite, gestures with her other hand, and says, "I went really long,"
Monica runs toward them, clapping her hands.
Monica makes an umpire's "safe" gesture with her arms and says, "42 to 21, like the turkey, ross is done."
Ross turns his head, shaking it slightly.
Ross points at Monica and says, "It's no surprise that you're winning because you got to pick first so you got the better team." Monica replies, "You're so pathetic. Why can't you just"
"accept it? We're winning because I'm better than you." Ross steps forward.
Ross looks at Monica.
Ross says, "Oh, what a great argument."
Monica puts her hands on her hips.
Monica breathes out forcefully and says, "Exhaling."
Ross slightly turns his head.
Ross gestures at Monica and says, "All right, you know what? I'll prove it to you, okay? I'll trade you Joey for Rachel, and I'll still win the"
"game." Rachel, holding her pretzel, says, "That's great. There goes my last shred of self esteem."
Ross turns his attention back to Monica.
Ross gestures with both hands and asks, "What? The guys against the girls?"
Ross says, "See, that's ridiculous, Monica, because I'm only down by three touchdowns." Monica smiles, spreads her arms wide, and says, "Then bring it on. Oh,"
Monica points a finger forward and continues, "unless, of course, you're afraid you might lose to a bunch of"

On an outdoor court scattered with autumn leaves, Ross stands facing off-screen left, with Chandler behind him. Monica's voice is heard off-screen, saying, "unless, of course, you're afraid you might lose to a bunch of"
Ross gestures forward and says, "fine. Fine."
Gesturing to his left, Ross says, "Rachel. You're with Monica."
Ross turns to his right, while Chandler and Rachel step into view behind him.
Ross points to his right and says, "Joey, you're with me."
Rachel steps forward, holding a partially eaten pretzel. "I cannot believe you are trading me," she says to Ross. Raising the pretzel, she adds, "You are gonna lose."
Rachel points the pretzel at Ross again. "And you are gonna lose and come on, help me out here."
She turns toward Monica, who stands nearby with hands on her hips. "I've never done this before." Monica says, "That's"
Monica nods and says, "good. Lose." Rachel turns back to Ross and repeats the pointing gesture with her pretzel.
Joey, Chandler, and Ross stand together, looking toward the women.
Monica claps her hands and walks past Rachel, saying, "Come on, Rach. Come on. Let's see what it's like to be on a winning team for a change."
Rachel turns to Monica and asks, "You're gonna let me play?" Monica looks at her. Rachel smiles and says, "All right, then"
Margha walks onto the court, approaching the men.
Margha walks toward Joey, Chandler, and Ross. "the game is over." she says.
"We eat," Margha continues. Chandler steps forward, gesturing with both hands. "No, the game's not over. We're just switching teams."
Joey steps up next to him, saying, "Yet Chandler finds me so intimidating that it's better"
Joey points at Chandler and says, "if we're on the same team, right?" Ross steps forward, clapping his hands, and says, "Okay, let's play. Let's go."
Chandler raises a hand toward Joey and says, "Hold on a sec, Joe."
Chandler points to Margha, then back to Joey, asking, "Where did Dutch people come from?"
Joey looks around, his mouth slightly open, blinking. Ross stands beside him, resting his hands on his hips.
Joey looks down, then slowly brings his gaze back up to Chandler, his facial expression remaining largely unchanged.
Joey points his finger forward and says, "Well, the Pennsylvania Dutch come from Pennsylvania."
Chandler keeps his arms crossed, staring at Joey.
Joey gestures with his hands, shifting his weight. "And the other Dutch people, they come from somewhere near the Netherlands, right?"
Chandler says, "Nice try."
Joey walks toward Chandler and Margha, gesturing with his hands.
Joey gestures expansively with both hands. "See, the Netherlands is this make believe place where Peter Pan and Tinkerbell come from."
Margha smiles, looking at Joey.
Margha says, "Oh, my."
Ross steps between Joey and Chandler.
Ross says, "Enough with geography for the insane, okay? Let's play some." Joey waves his hand and turns away.
"No, no. I'm not playing with this guy," Joey says. Ross points at Joey and says, "Oh, fine with"

Ross continues, "me. Okay, you know what? I've had enough of this. Let's just cut to the chase here, okay?" Ross places his arms around Joey and Chandler's shoulders, facing Margha. "Heidi, which of my boys do you like?"
Margha stands with her hands on her hips, looking at the men.
Chandler and Joey both start talking simultaneously, stepping away from Ross. "What are you doing? What are you doing? What are you doing?" they ask, gesturing with their hands.
Margha speaks briefly to the men.
Ross gestures with his hands and says, "Which do you know for dating general merriment? Taking back to your windmill?"
Margha stands with her hands on her hips and says, "If I had to choose right now"
She continues, "which, by the way, I find really weird,"
Margha pauses, looking at the men.
Margha continues, "I would have to say"
Margha extends her hand, pointing toward Chandler. Chandler's eyes widen as he points to his own chest.
Joey points at Margha and says, "now wait a minute, wait a minute. She obviously didn't understand the question."
Chandler gestures toward Joey and says, "Well, why don't you have Captain Hook explain it to her?"
Margha speaks to Joey.
Margha says, "I'm sorry, Joey. That is my truth."
Chandler points at Joey. "You hear that? That is her truth. Mr. I'll let you have her. I win. You suck."
Chandler raises his arms and moves his hips, chanting, "I rule. A mini wave in celebration of me."
Chandler continues waving his hands in the air, then stops and turns his attention back to Margha.
Margha looks at Chandler and says, "I am now thinking I would like to change my answer to no one."
Chandler's smile fades as he stares at Margha.
Chandler asks, "What?"
Margha says, "I now find you shallow and a dork."
Margha turns away from the group.
Margha walks away, waving a hand and saying, "Bye bye."
Joey watches her walk away, then turns to face Chandler.
Ross steps toward Chandler and says, "Nice going."
Joey smiles at Chandler and says, "You just saved yourself a couple of months of sex."
Chandler places his hands on his hips.
Chandler says, "You know what? Doesn't matter. Because she picked me." He points a finger at his own chest.
Chandler steps toward Joey, pointing at him. "And me. From now on, I get the dates."
Chandler continues, "And you have to stay home on Saturday nights watching Ready, Set, Cook."
Ross steps between Chandler and Joey.
Ross pushes Chandler and Joey apart with his hands. "Save the breakthroughs for therapy, okay? The clock is ticking. We have no time, and we are losing. We are losing to"
Joey points at Ross and says, "girls. We're not going to lose to girls." Ross replies, "It's 42 to 21."
Joey looks down, shaking his head slightly, and says, "Sucks. I was just up by that much."
Joey continues looking down as Ross stares at him.
In an outdoor dirt field scattered with autumn leaves, Ross, Chandler, and Joey stand together.
Ross points toward Chandler and says, "Look, let me put it this way. If we lose, they will never let us forget it."

Ross points at Joey, adding, "Okay? And worse than that, they'll never let the women you date forget it." In the distance, Monica gestures with her arm and shouts, "Hey, are we playing football or what? Come on,"
Monica continues shouting, "you hairy-backed Marys," as Phoebe and Rachel stand beside her.
The women gather into a huddle.
Monica leans into the huddle, moving her hands as she speaks. "we have to do this. We are playing for women everywhere. Okay? Just think about every lousy date you ever had, okay? Every guy who"
Monica continues, "kept one eye on the TV while you were making out." Phoebe leans forward and asks, "Oh, my God. You dated someone with a glass eye too?"
Monica stares silently at Phoebe.
Monica clenches her fists and shakes them slightly. "Come on, okay? Come on. This is for all womankind."
Monica says, "Let's kill them." Phoebe and Rachel raise their fists and say, "Yeah, kill them."
Phoebe and Rachel lower their hands.
Phoebe holds the football in front of her. "All right. Well, no, I mean, I want to kill them too, but they're boys. How are we going to beat three boys?"
Chandler runs while holding the football. Phoebe runs toward him, stopping abruptly with her back to him. She lifts the front of her shirt, exposing her midriff and back.
Chandler smiles and lowers his arms as Phoebe grabs the football from his hands; an uncertain speaker says, "You."
Phoebe runs away with the football while Chandler stands still, smiling. Phoebe turns and tosses the ball forward.
The ball flies through the air. Monica and Joey run to catch it, with Joey intercepting the ball.
Joey runs forward holding the ball. Monica jumps onto Joey's back, wrapping her arms around his neck.
Rachel grabs Monica's legs and is dragged across the ground. Joey continues running forward.
Joey extends his arm and tosses the ball away. Phoebe catches the ball and runs, with Chandler chasing her.
Phoebe crashes backward into a large bush. Chandler stops and places his hands on his hips. The women stand bent over, breathing heavily.
Monica, Rachel, and Phoebe huddle again, keeping their hands on their knees. Monica says, "We still have a minute and a half left to go, and we're down by two points. Okay, two points, ladies."
In the background, Ross, Chandler, and Joey raise their arms and cheer. Monica turns her head away from the men and looks at Phoebe and Rachel.
Monica says, "Phoebe, you do a buttonhook again."
Monica points at Rachel. "Rachel, you go long." Rachel shakes her head and gestures with her hands. "No, come on. Don't make me go long."
Rachel taps her chest. "Use me. They never cover me." Monica points at Rachel and replies, "Honey, there's a reason"
Rachel keeps her hands near her chest, her mouth slightly open.

Rachel gestures with both hands. "I'm not lame, okay? I can do something. I can throw. Would you let me throw? Come on, this is my game too." Phoebe looks at Monica and says, "Come on, mom, let her throw the ball."
Monica says, "All right, Rachel, you sweep behind. I'll pitch it to you. You throw it downfield to Phoebe."
Rachel smiles and says, "Thank you."
The women stack their hands together in the center of the huddle.
The women pull their hands apart and say, "Break. Break."
The teams take their positions. Monica bends over the football while Ross, Chandler, and Joey stand opposite her in a defensive stance.
Monica shouts, "32, 71, hike." She picks up the ball and pitches it backward.
Rachel catches the pitch. Ross, Chandler, and Joey rush toward her shouting. Rachel screams, turns, and throws the ball into the air without looking. An uncertain speaker says, "Gosh."
Rachel runs out of view. The men stop running, and Ross holds his head. Monica stands with her hands on her hips.
Rachel stands rubbing her eye. Monica walks over to her.
Monica touches Rachel's shoulder and asks, "Are you okay?" Rachel continues rubbing her eye and says, "No, I'm not okay."
Rachel keeps her hand on her face.
Rachel says, "Sorry. They were just all coming at me and I didn't know what to do." Joey holds up a small timer. "30 seconds left on the timer." Someone off-screen shouts, "Woohoo."
Joey holds the timer in the air and smiles.
Joey performs a rhythmic dance, raising his knees and pointing his fingers while singing, "You are the loser"
Ross and Chandler join Joey in the rhythmic dance, stepping side to side.
Ross claps his hands. "okay, so we get to take that stupid troll thing home." Monica gestures toward Phoebe and Rachel. "Come on, come on. Hurry. We're running out of time. Huddle up."
Monica adds, "Okay,"
The women form another huddle.
Phoebe looks down and says, "this is our last huddle."
Monica rubs her eye.
Monica gestures with her hand. "All right, phoebe, get open. Rachel, go long."
Rachel nods and says, "Okay, great."
The women approach the line of scrimmage while the men get into defensive positions. Monica bends over the football.
Monica picks up the ball and drops back several steps, holding the ball up and scanning the field.
Chandler, Ross, and Joey rush forward. Monica steps forward and throws the football down the field.
The football arcs through the air. Rachel runs across the leaf-covered field, extending her arms overhead to catch the falling ball.
In a leaf-strewn park, Monica holds a football as upbeat music plays. Ross screams and sprints toward her.
Ross closes the distance, and Monica looks startled, trying to turn away.
Ross tackles Monica to the ground, and the football flies up into the air.
Rachel looks up, tracking the ball with her arms raised. Ross and Monica watch from the ground.
Joey, Phoebe, and Chandler watch intently. Rachel steps back and catches the falling football.
Rachel runs forward holding the ball. Ross and Monica look on from the ground. Joey, Phoebe, and Chandler cheer.
Rachel jumps up and down with the ball, laughing.
The group runs toward Rachel and hugs her. The music stops. Chandler then turns to Rachel.
Chandler gestures to Rachel and says, "Rachel?"
Chandler continues, "Funny thing, actually."
Chandler points toward the fence and says, "The end zone starts at that pole."
Chandler says, "So you're 5ft short. So we" as he and the men high-five.
The men cheer and celebrate. Phoebe steps forward, pointing at them.
Phoebe says, "wait. So explain something to me, though."
Phoebe continues, "If nobody tagged Rachel, then isn't the play still going?"
The group looks at the football on the ground. Everyone scrambles and dives onto the ball in a pile.
The others stand up, leaving Ross and Monica struggling on the ground, both gripping the football.
Monica says, "Let go of a tiny little woman."
Chandler approaches the pair on the ground.
Chandler gestures and says, "Guys, come on. It's Thanksgiving. It's not important who wins or loses."
Chandler points to himself and adds, "The important thing is the Dutch girl picked me."
Chandler points a finger at Joey.
Chandler says, "Me, not you. Holland loves Chandler. Thank you, Amsterdam. Good night," raising his arms and then walking away.
In an apartment living room, Chandler, Joey, Rachel, and Phoebe sit around a coffee table eating Thanksgiving dinner.
Rachel holds a plate and eats.
Rachel says, "We should definitely play football more often."
Rachel continues, "Maybe there's, like, a league we could join or something."
Joey and Chandler look up from their food.
Phoebe asks, "Isn't there, like, a national football league?"
Chandler replies, "Yes, there is. They play on Sundays and Monday nights." Rachel says, "Oh,"
Rachel finishes, "shoot. I work Monday nights."
Phoebe continues eating.
Phoebe says, "That thing is amazing. Do you think we should bring them some?"
Joey replies, "When they're hungry enough, they'll come in." The scene changes to the dark park, where Ross and Monica still lie on the ground holding the ball. Ross says, "Let go."
Monica says, "No, you let go."
Ross says, "No,"
Ross and Monica continue to lie face-to-face on the dark field.
Ross says, "it's always us left on the field, holding the ball."
Monica replies, "I don't know. Guess the other people just don't care enough."
Ross and Monica remain on the ground. Snow begins to fall around them.
Ross looks up and says, "It, hey, it's starting to snow."
They look at each other. Ross starts pulling the ball, and they resume struggling.
Ross says, "Give us." Monica says, "Don't give it."
Ross and Monica continue struggling on the ground.
The scene changes to an apartment hallway. Monica and Ross walk toward a door, holding the troll doll trophy together.
Monica says, "You know, there was a time tonight that I started losing the feeling of my toes." Ross replies, "I know."
Monica says, "That's when I realized there's more important things than beating you at football."
Ross looks at Monica.
Monica says, "Things like feet."
Ross lifts the trophy and says, "I know, this thing has caused too much pain and suffering. It's time"
Monica says, "we'll just call it a tie." Ross agrees, "It's a tie."
They shake hands. Ross pulls open the trash chute door.
Ross says, "Okay."
They throw the doll into the chute, and Ross closes the door. They turn away.
Ross says, "Good night."
Monica says, "Good night."
They walk away toward their respective apartment doors.

In an apartment hallway, Monica jogs to a metal trash chute set into the wall. A blue sign above a yellow recycling bin reads "PLEASE PUT RECYCLED OBJECTS IN PROPER BINS. Thank you, MGMT." White production credits appear on screen.
Monica pulls open the chute door and leans her face near the opening. "Phoebe, did you get it?" she asks, her voice echoing.
Phoebe's voice, muffled from below, replies, "Yeah, I got it," followed by audience laughter.
Monica leans toward the opening. "Great. Bring it up. There's champagne in my apartment for the winners," she says. She stands upright, smiling, and lets the door close.
Monica's smile fades as she turns her head sharply. She then grips the chute handle again, pulls the door open, and leans back in, looking downward.
"What are you doing down here?" Monica calls down the chute.
Ross's muffled voice stammers briefly from below, prompting audience laughter, as Monica's jaw drops open slightly.
Ross says, "Nothing."
Audience laughter continues as Monica scowls and groans, keeping her gaze directed down the chute. The visual and audio then cut to black.
The screen and audio remain completely black and silent.
The black screen and silence continue.
